dar trabalho.reason: sent2_span 'dá bastante trabalho' is not the item's surface form: it includes the extra intensifier 'bastante' inserted between 'dá' and 'trabalho', making the span a longer phrase than the item 'dar trabalho' (rule 4 violation). sent1 has the analogous issue ('dando muito trabalho' with 'muito' inserted), so the item never actually appears as a clean contiguous chunk in either sentence.

ficar (tornar-se).reason: sent2_span 'ficou muito quieto' is far longer than the item's inflected surface form ('ficou'); it swallows the entire predicate (intensifier + adjective). Removing it leaves 'Depois da notícia, ele ___.' with zero recoverable cue — the resulting state (quiet, angry, sad, happy, anything) cannot be inferred from what remains, so the blank fails the recoverability test as well as the surface-form rule.

pegar mal.reason: sent2_span is 'pega muito mal', which inserts 'muito' — a word not part of the item. The span must be the item's own inflected surface form ('pega mal' / 'pegou mal'), not a longer phrase padded with an intensifier.

pegar pesado.reason: sent2_span is 'pegou muito pesado', which inserts 'muito' beyond the item's inflected surface form ('pegou pesado'). The span is a longer phrase than the item, violating the exact-surface-form rule.

pegar uma cola.reason: The chunk itself is off: Brazilians don't say 'pegar uma cola' to mean 'to cheat with a cheat sheet' — the standard verb is 'colar' (colar na prova = to cheat on the test), with 'cola' as the noun for the cheat sheet used with 'usar'. sent2 'Ela quase pegou uma cola no exame de química' reads as 'she almost obtained/got hold of a cheat sheet', not 'she almost cheated' — a semantic mismatch with the gloss, not a genuine synonym for colar.

passar mal.reason: sent2_span is 'passei muito mal', which inserts 'muito' — not part of the item's surface form ('passei mal'). The span exceeds the item, same defect pattern as 'pegar mal' and 'pegar pesado' in this batch.

passar a mão na cabeça (de).reason: sent1 'O juiz não devia passar a mão na cabeça de criminoso' drops the article/number on 'criminoso' — no Brazilian says it this way in speech; natural phrasing would be 'do criminoso' (singular+article) or 'de criminosos' (plural). As written it reads like clipped headline text, not spoken Portuguese.

deixar a desejar.reason: sent2 minus the blank is just 'A qualidade do produto ____.' — the entire predicate carrying the idiom's meaning is inside the span, leaving zero contextual cue to recover it from (matches the explicit 'sentence whose whole meaning was inside the blank' failure case).

jogar conversa fora.reason: sent2 'Vamos jogar conversa fora enquanto espera o ônibus?' mismatches subject: 'vamos' is 1st person plural but 'espera' is 3rd person singular with no subject established (should be 'esperamos' or 'a gente espera') — a real speaker wouldn't switch person mid-sentence like this.

jogar verde para colher maduro.reason: sent2 minus sent2_span leaves only 'Fica esperto, ela tá .' — a dangling auxiliary with no predicate; the entire informative content of the sentence is inside the blank, giving no recoverable cue for the idiom.

The full idiom 'jogar verde para colher maduro' is a written/proverbial expression, not something Brazilians actually say out loud in casual conversation — spoken usage almost always shortens to just 'jogar verde'. Both example sentences plug the full bookish idiom into otherwise casual speech ('tá', 'pra'), which reads as textbook-stitched rather than authentic.

tomar conta (de).reason: Removing sent2_span ('tomar conta das crianças') leaves 'Quem vai hoje à noite?', a fully complete, natural standalone question — nothing signals a verb phrase is missing, so the blank is not recoverable from context.

tomar um susto.reason: sent2_span 'tomou um susto enorme' is longer than the item (adds 'enorme'), and removing it leaves the ungrammatical fragment 'Ela com o barulho da porta.' (no verb at all) — unrecoverable.

tomar uma.reason: Removing sent2_span leaves 'Ele foi com os amigos no bar.', a complete natural sentence (he went to the bar with friends) with no signal that a drink-related verb was elided.

a gente tá cansado.reason: sent2_span 'a gente tá cansado demais' is longer than the item (adds 'demais'), and removing it leaves the broken fragment 'Depois do jogo pra sair.' — no subject, no verb, unrecoverable.

a gente (pronome geral).reason: Removing 'a gente' leaves 'No trabalho tem que chegar cedo sempre.', a fully grammatical, complete impersonal sentence on its own — nothing cues that 'a gente' belongs there.

tem muita gente aqui.reason: sent2_span 'tem muita gente aqui na fila' is longer than the item (adds 'na fila'), and removing it leaves only 'Não vou entrar,' with a dangling comma and no specific content cue.

não tem ninguém.reason: sent2_span 'não tem ninguém na sala' is longer than the item 'não tem ninguém' — 'na sala' is not part of the item and shouldn't be included in the blanked span.

quando eu for.reason: sent2_span 'quando eu for embora' adds 'embora' beyond the item 'quando eu for', and removing it leaves 'Te aviso daqui.', a complete standalone sentence with a shifted meaning and no cue a temporal clause was elided.

se eu tiver tempo.reason: sent2_span 'se eu tiver tempo à noite' is longer than the item (adds 'à noite'), and removing it leaves 'A gente vai no cinema.', a fully complete sentence with zero indication a conditional clause was there.

assim que ele chegar.reason: sent2_span 'assim que ele chegar em casa' is longer than the item (adds 'em casa'), and removing it leaves just 'Me liga.' — the degenerate no-cue case the rules define as an automatic failure.

ao sairmos.reason: 'Ao + infinitivo pessoal' ('Ao sairmos do cinema...', 'Ao sairmos de casa...') is written/literary register. A Brazilian narrating this out loud says 'Quando a gente saiu do cinema' / 'Na hora que saímos de casa', not 'ao sairmos'. Textbook-correct but not something spoken casually.

ficar (permanecer).reason: sent2_span 'vai ficar' is longer than the item's own surface form: the item is the single verb 'ficar', but the span bundles in 'vai' (a conjugated form of the separate verb 'ir', used for the periphrastic future), which is not part of the item at all. Violates rule 4 (span must be the item's inflected form, not a longer phrase). Compare to the correctly-scoped spans for the sibling 'ficar' senses ('fica', 'ficou bom') and for 'estar com X' items where the extra word ('tava'/'tá') is legitimately part of the multi-word item itself.

ficar bom (de saúde).reason: cuidado claim is incoherent: '⚠️ No es "ponerse bien" en sentido moral' misattributes a 'moral sense' ambiguity to the Spanish phrase 'ponerse bien', which in Spanish is itself the standard way to say 'to get better/recover (health)' and carries no moral connotation. The real ambiguity (recover health vs. become a good person) belongs to Portuguese 'ficar bom', not to the Spanish equivalent, so the cuidado misrepresents what Spanish 'ponerse bien' means.

estar com medo.reason: sent2_span 'fica com medo' uses the verb 'ficar' (fica), not an inflected form of 'estar' as the item names it; the item is 'estar com medo' but no form of 'estar' appears anywhere in sent2. estar com pressa.reason: sent2_span 'fica com pressa' uses 'ficar' (fica), not a surface form of 'estar', which is the verb named in the item 'estar com pressa'. Same estar/ficar mismatch as the other entries in this micro-set (rule 4).

estar com razão.reason: sent2_span 'fica com razão' is built on 'ficar', not 'estar' as specified by the item 'estar com razão'; span is not an inflected form of the item's own verb (rule 4).

estar com calor/frio.reason: sent2_span 'ficaram com frio' uses 'ficar', not 'estar' as named in the item 'estar com calor/frio'. Rule 4 violation: no form of 'estar' occurs in sent2 at all.

né (pergunta de confirmação).reason: sent2_span is 'hoje, né', which bundles in the preceding word 'hoje' even though 'né' alone already occurs exactly once in sent2 and would suffice. Rule 4 violation: span is a longer phrase than the item 'né'.

acordar.reason: sent2 'Ela sempre acorda com o despertador tocando três vezes' is stilted — a real Brazilian describing a heavy sleeper needing multiple alarm rings would say something like 'Ela só acorda depois que o despertador toca umas três vezes' or 'Ela demora pra acordar, o despertador toca três vezes até ela levantar'. The 'acorda com X tocando três vezes' phrasing reads as a translated construction, not natural spoken BR.

Rule 4 violation: span wraps in the subject 'você' and verb 'mora', which are not part of the item, and removing all three leaves only 'Nossa, _____ naquele apartamento pequeno?' — too little targeted cue for the single word being tested.

anedota.reason: sent2_span is 'Essa anedota que', but the item is just 'anedota'. Rule 4 violation: span includes the demonstrative 'Essa' and relative pronoun 'que', which are not part of the item.

apelido.reason: sent2_span is 'tem um apelido', but the item is just 'apelido'. Rule 4 violation: span includes the verb 'tem' and article 'um', which are not part of the item.

apenas.reason: sent2_span is 'Faltam apenas dois', but the item is just 'apenas'. Rule 4 violation: span includes 'Faltam' and 'dois', which are not part of the item.

balcão.reason: sent2 'Pergunta pro balcão se já chegou minha encomenda' personifies the counter as if it were a person you address directly ('pro balcão' = 'to the counter'); a Brazilian would say 'pergunta lá no balcão' or 'pergunta pro pessoal do balcão' — 'balcão' isn't a job title like 'caixa' that doubles as a person, so addressing it directly with 'pro' sounds off.

constipado.reason: sent2_span is 'tá constipado', which appends the copula 'tá' beyond the item's own surface form ('constipado'). Rule 4 requires the span to be the item's inflected surface form, not a longer phrase; the extra verb widens the tested unit past what 'item' specifies.

bater.reason: sent2_span is 'bateu o carro', which bundles the object 'o carro' onto the inflected verb — a longer phrase than the item's surface form (rule 4). Removing the whole span also strips the object needed to recover the 'crash' sense: 'Ele ___ na esquina ontem à noite' could as easily be 'parou'/'virou'/etc., so the blank is weakly recoverable (rule 5).

botar.reason: sent2_span is 'botou a chave', pairing the inflected verb with its object noun — longer than the item's own surface form (rule 4). With the object folded into the blank, 'Ela ___ dentro da bolsa e esqueceu' no longer clearly signals 'put' over other placement/loss verbs, weakening recoverability (rule 5).

doce.reason: sent2_span is 'doce de leite', a specific 3-word compound (dulce de leche) rather than the item's own surface form 'doce' — violates rule 4 (longer phrase, different/narrower sense than the plain word being taught).

embaraçado.reason: sent1 'Fiquei todo embaraçado quando ela elogiou na frente de todo mundo' drops the direct object of 'elogiar' — a transitive verb needing a complement. No Brazilian would say bare 'ela elogiou' meaning 'she complimented (me)'; it needs 'ela me elogiou'. As written it reads as an unfinished/ungrammatical sentence, not natural spoken Portuguese.

fechar.reason: cuidado wrongly implies Spanish 'fechar' isn't used for 'poner fecha' (dating a document) and that only 'datar' works. In fact Spanish 'fechar' is a standard verb meaning 'to date/put a date on' (e.g. 'fechar una carta'), so the parenthetical '(eso es datar)' misstates Spanish vocabulary even though the core PT/ES semantic gap (cerrar vs fechar-fecha) is real.

galho.reason: sent2_span is 'dois galhos grossos' — a full noun phrase (numeral + noun + adjective), not the item's inflected surface form. Rule 4 requires the span to be just the inflected item, not a longer phrase.

ninguém.reason: sent2_span is 'e ninguém atendeu' — includes the conjunction 'e' and the verb 'atendeu' around the item, not just the inflected surface form of 'ninguém'. Rule 4 violation; also weakens recoverability since the verb that would normally cue the answer is blanked out too.

paquete.reason: "Paquete" is not a recognizable Brazilian slang term for menstruation (common real ones are 'chico'/'tio Chico', 'baixo-astral', 'período', 'menstruação'); this reads as a fabricated or non-Brazilian term, and both sentences ('tá de paquete', 'veio o paquete') would not actually be understood/said by a Brazilian, which defeats the deck's core listening-comprehension purpose.

Unable to verify 'paquete' as genuine Brazilian slang for menstruation; known BR-PT euphemisms are 'chico'/'tio Chico chegou', 'regra', 'está naqueles dias', etc. Not 'paquete'. The item, gloss, and both example sentences all hinge on this unverified/likely-fabricated slang sense, and the vulgar/rare hedge in notes reads as cover for an invented entry — defaulting to fail per uncertainty rule since a wrong vocabulary item here is high-risk.

película.reason: sent2_span 'película do olho' blanks out the disambiguating noun 'olho' itself, leaving 'A ___ fica irritada com o vento' with no cue pointing to a thin film/membrane specifically — could be skin, eyes, face, allergies; not recoverable.

Both example sentences use unnatural collocations. Brazilians don't say 'película de plástico' for cling wrap over food — the standard term is 'filme plástico' or 'papel filme'; 'película' is used for phone screen protectors, car window tinting, or photographic film, not kitchen wrap. Likewise 'a película do olho fica irritada com o vento' is a constructed-sounding anatomical phrase nobody actually says; a real speaker would just say 'o olho fica irritado com o vento'.

podre.reason: sent2_span 'cheiro de podre' swallows 'cheiro' (the smell cue), leaving 'O ___ tomou conta da geladeira' — too generic (mess, mold, bugs, smell) to recover 'podre' specifically.

pó.reason: sent2 uses the reversed, non-standard word order 'pó de leite'. The natural Brazilian collocation is 'leite em pó' (powdered milk) — 'pó de leite' sounds backward and is not how a native speaker would say it.

saltar.reason: sent1 teaches 'saltar do ônibus' for 'to get off the bus', but this is European Portuguese usage. In Brazil the verb for disembarking is 'descer' ('descer do ônibus') — 'saltar' in Brazilian speech means only 'to jump' (as correctly shown in sent2), not 'to alight'. The gloss/definition and sent1 import a Portugal-only sense as if it were Brazilian.

taco.reason: sent2_span 'taco de golfe' blanks the only disambiguating word 'golfe', leaving 'O ___ dela é importado' — a generic possessive statement with zero cue toward a golf club or pool cue (could be any imported object).

vassoura.reason: sent2_span 'A vassoura nova' blanks the entire subject, leaving only 'tá atrás da porta' (is behind the door) — no semantic cue survives to recover that the missing word is a broom; the remainder fits almost any noun phrase.

todavia.reason: sent2_span 'todavia foi' bundles the connective with the unrelated main verb 'foi' (from 'foi trabalhar' = went to work). Blanking both leaves 'Ele tava cansado, ___ trabalhar', which is ungrammatical/unrecoverable; the span should be just 'todavia', leaving 'Ele tava cansado, ___ foi trabalhar' which is clean and recoverable via the contrastive comma structure.

sent2 mixes a reduced spoken verb form ('tava') with 'todavia', which the deck's own notes admit is 'mostly formal/written register' rarely used in casual speech ('people usually just say mas or só que'). No real Brazilian says 'Ele tava cansado, todavia foi trabalhar' out loud — the literary conjunction clashes with the colloquial contraction in the same breath. Sent1 is equally stiff/written-register, undermining a deck whose whole point is spoken naturalness.

presunto.reason: sent2_span 'Comprei presunto fatiado' blanks the verb and adjective along with the item, leaving only 'no mercado' (at the market). That remainder gives no cue at all about what was bought — could be any product — so the blank is not recoverable, and the span is far longer than the item's surface form ('presunto').

latido.reason: sent2_span 'O latido ficou' blanks subject and verb together, leaving only 'mais forte de noite' (got louder at night). That fits almost any noun (barulho, vento, chuva, medo...), so there is no cue recoverable to 'latido' specifically.

cadeira.reason: sent2_span 'cadeira da cozinha' blanks the noun plus its only distinguishing modifier ('da cozinha'), leaving 'A ___ tá quebrada.' — nothing but 'something is broken' remains, which fits any household object equally well as 'chair'; not recoverable.

bolso.reason: sent2_span 'caiu do bolso' blanks the entire predicate, leaving 'Cadê minha carteira? Acho que ___.' — no lexical trace of 'fell' or 'pocket' remains, so a listener cannot recover 'bolso' specifically over any other explanation for a missing wallet (car, home, lost, etc.).

mano.reason: sent2_span 'Mano, essa prova' bleeds into the next clause and swallows 'essa prova' (the subject of 'foi difícil'), which has nothing to do with the item; this is a longer/misaligned phrase, not the item's surface form, and leaves 'foi difícil demais' with zero cue for either the vocative or the missing subject.

largar.reason: sent2_span 'largou o namorado' is a longer phrase than the item's surface form: item is the bare verb 'largar' with no parenthetical marking a multi-word target, yet the span pulls in the direct object 'o namorado' too. Removing it leaves 'Ela ___ depois da viagem.' which gives no cue to recover the object, only that something happened after the trip.

The 'cuidado' claims rioplatense Spanish uses 'largar' in a more limited way than Portuguese. This is doubtful: rioplatense Spanish colloquially uses 'largar' very broadly and similarly to the Portuguese senses given here — 'largar el laburo' (quit the job), 'largar a la novia/al novio' (dump the partner), 'largá eso' (drop/let go of something) are all common. The claimed contrast with Portuguese usage is not verifiable and likely overstated/false.

arrumar.reason: sent2_span 'arrumar um trampo' extends beyond the item's own surface form (item is just 'arrumar', no parenthetical licensing a multi-word phrase) by also swallowing the direct object 'um trampo' - which is itself another deck item ('trampo'), making the blank test two vocabulary items at once instead of just 'arrumar'.

tipo.reason: sent1 'Ele é tipo muito estressado com o trabalho ultimamente' clashes 'é' (permanent trait, ser) with 'ultimamente' (implies a recent/temporary state) — a real Brazilian describing a recent stressed patch says 'tá' or 'anda' tipo estressado, not 'é'.

pra mim.reason: The 'notes' and 'cuidado' claim 'pra mim' is informally used 'as subject' where standard grammar requires 'eu' (citing 'pra mim tá bom'), but neither the card's own cited example nor sent2 ('Pra mim, esse plano não vai dar certo') actually illustrates that phenomenon — in both, 'pra mim' is a fronted dative/opinion-marker ('to me' / 'in my opinion'), with 'esse plano' or an implicit 'isso' as the real subject, not 'mim'. The genuine BP colloquialism (using 'mim' instead of 'eu' as subject of an infinitive, e.g. 'pra mim chegar lá vai ser difícil') requires a following infinitive, which is absent from every example given. The cuidado's blanket claim that Spanish 'para mí' 'nunca es sujeto de oración' while Portuguese 'sí puede usarse así' is therefore not demonstrated and is misleading as written, since the opinion-marker sense shown ('Para mí, este plan no va a funcionar') is perfectly normal, identical-structure Spanish too.

pela.reason: sent1 'A gente foi pela praia até o final da tarde' mixes the route-preposition 'pela' (going along/through) with a duration phrase ('até o final da tarde'); a real Brazilian describing spending the afternoon at the beach would say 'A gente ficou na praia até o final da tarde' or 'A gente foi pra praia de manhã e ficou até a tarde' — 'foi pela praia...até' reads as constructed to showcase the word, not natural spoken usage.

vô.reason: sent2 'Amanhã vô lá em casa te ajudar' is semantically inconsistent: 'lá em casa' is the fixed deictic expression for the SPEAKER's own home ('at my place'), not the listener's. A Brazilian offering to go help someone at THEIR place would say 'vou aí (na tua casa) te ajudar' or 'passo aí pra te ajudar', not 'lá em casa'. As written, the sentence literally reads 'Tomorrow I'll go to my house to help you,' which doesn't parse the way sent2_en claims ('I'll come by your place').

sent2/sent2_en mismatch: 'Amanhã vô lá em casa te ajudar' uses the fixed idiom 'lá em casa' which means 'at MY (the speaker's) house', not 'your place'. The literal sense is 'Tomorrow I'll go home to help you', which contradicts the given translation 'Tomorrow I'll come by your place to help you' — the direction of movement is reversed.

memo.reason: Verified: Spanish 'memo/-a' is a real colloquial adjective meaning 'tonto/bobo' (fool), so the cuidado claim is true and correctly contrasted with BR Portuguese 'memo' = colloquial 'mesmo' (intensifier).
